skills.skill: Counting to 1,000: Learn to count sequentially up to 1,000 through number charts and counting exercises.
skills.prompt: This topic covers Counting to 1,000: Learn to count sequentially up to 1,000 through number charts and counting exercises. for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.2(C)). Students develop counting fluency, understanding the number sequence and its patterns. Key concepts include forward/backward counting, skip counting, place value patterns, and real-world counting. Vocabulary: count, number, sequence, pattern, skip count, forward, backward. Common misconceptions include decade transition errors and losing track. Activities should include manipulatives, charts, games, and real-world tasks appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, build from concrete manipulatives to pictorial representations to abstract notation, ensuring 7-8-year-olds develop both conceptual understanding and procedural fluency.
